rubric: See ground_truth/RUBRIC.md — label for DURATION budgeting, not literary theory.

labels.setting: EXT. STREETS OF ST. PETERSBURGH - DAY
labels.visual_event: Late 18th century. Snow packed hard underfoot. A cold northern breeze cuts the air. ROBERT WALTON, twenty-eight, lean and bright-eyed, walks in heavy furs. The wind strikes his cheeks. He closes his eyes a moment, smiling, braced and delighted. He looks north, as if he can already see the pole.

labels.setting: INT. WALTON'S LODGING - ST. PETERSBURGH - NIGHT
labels.visual_event: Walton writes on, fervor rising.

next_beat.dialogue: These are my enticements, and they are sufficient to conquer all fear of danger …

labels.setting: INT. WALTON'S LODGING - ST. PETERSBURGH - NIGHT
labels.visual_event: Back to the letter. Walton's face is open, almost pleading with the page.

next_beat.dialogue: And now, dear Margaret, do I not deserve to accomplish some great purpose?

labels.visual_event: He stops. The room is empty. The silence presses.

prev_beat.dialogue: I have hired a vessel and am occupied in collecting my sailors.

next_beat.dialogue: But I have one want which I have never yet been able to satisfy.

labels.visual_event: He seals the letter.
Victor wrapped in blankets, fevered, slowly mending under Walton's care. Walton sits by him with broth and quiet talk.

next_beat.dialogue: I never saw a more interesting creature.

labels.visual_event: A modest carriage pulls away from Geneva's walls. Beaufort does not look back. Caroline steadies him.

labels.setting: EXT. SHORES OF THE LAKE OF COMO - DAY
labels.visual_event: Years later. Young Victor, about five-slim, dark hair, intent curious face-walks with Caroline past poor cottages. Alphonse has gone ahead toward Milan. A disconsolate cot in a vale. Half-clothed children. Caroline's face softens with necessity and memory.
Morning. Caroline presents Elizabeth. Young Victor receives her with childish seriousness-as a possession of his own to protect, love, and cherish.

next_beat.dialogue: Cousin.

labels.visual_event: Victor's disappointment hardens into secret resolve.

prev_beat.dialogue: Ah! Cornelius Agrippa! My dear Victor, do not waste your time upon this; it is s…

next_beat.dialogue: If, instead of this remark, my father had taken the pains to explain that the pr…

labels.visual_event: Alphonse, broken but upright. Victor prepares for university. Elizabeth and Clerval bid him farewell with tears and forced cheer.

next_beat.dialogue: Write, Victor. Come home to us soon.

labels.visual_event: Candles burnt low. Victor still at work as the high steeple outside pales toward morning. Stars disappear in the light. His application no longer fluctuating-ardent, eager, alone.

next_beat.dialogue: From this day natural philosophy, and particularly chemistry, became nearly my s…

labels.visual_event: He closes the book. Resolve hardens.

prev_beat.dialogue: Whence, I often asked myself, did the principle of life proceed?

next_beat.dialogue: It was a bold question.

labels.visual_event: Walton leans forward, eagerness naked in his eyes. Victor meets that look and stops him with a raised hand.

next_beat.dialogue: I see by your eagerness and the wonder and hope which your eyes express, my frie…

labels.setting: INT. VICTOR'S WORKSHOP - TOP OF THE HOUSE - NIGHT
labels.visual_event: A solitary chamber, almost a cell, separated by gallery and stair. Filthy creation: instruments, vats, the vast unfinished form under cloth. Victor's eyeballs starting from their sockets as he works the details of fibre, muscle, vein. Rain at the high small window. His person emaciated; confinement written in every line of him.

next_beat.dialogue: In a solitary chamber, or rather cell, at the top of the house, I kept my worksh…

labels.visual_event: Victor wanders, unable to return. Morning light finds him exhausted near an inn. A coach rattles in. HENRY CLERVAL springs down, radiant.

next_beat.dialogue: My dear Frankenstein-how glad I am to see you!

labels.visual_event: Victor nods almost imperceptibly-memory of a frank, happy face.

prev_beat.dialogue: Justine, you may remember, was a great favourite of yours.

next_beat.dialogue: When my dearest aunt died, poor Justine attended her with the most anxious affec…

labels.setting: INT. M. WALDMAN'S STUDY - UNIVERSITY - DAY
labels.visual_event: Books, specimens, soft light. M. WALDMAN-kind, silvered, warm-clasps Victor's hand, then Henry's.

next_beat.dialogue: M. Frankenstein. Returned to us. And this is M. Clerval-welcome.

labels.setting: INT. UNIVERSITY CORRIDOR AND KREMPE'S ROOMS - DAY
labels.visual_event: Krempe launches into a eulogy on himself. Victor breathes again.

prev_beat.dialogue: Young men should be diffident of themselves, you know, M. Clerval.

labels.setting: EXT. COUNTRY ROAD NEAR INGOLSTADT - DAY
labels.visual_event: Victor turns, radiant, and throws an arm toward the open sky.
Thunder. In a flash of lightning Victor sees among the trees a gigantic figure-the Creature-climbing the rocks of a nearby precipice with superhuman speed.

next_beat.dialogue: It is he. The murderer of my brother.

labels.visual_event: A murmur of horror fills the court. Justine's countenance alters-surprise, horror, misery.

prev_beat.dialogue: It is the same which, an hour before the child had been missed, I placed round h…

next_beat.dialogue: God knows how entirely I am innocent. But I do not pretend that my protestations…

labels.visual_event: A murmur of approbation-excited by Elizabeth's generous interference, not in favour of Justine. Public indignation turns on the accused with renewed violence. Victor's face is a mask of agony. He can sustain it no longer. He rushes out of the court.

prev_beat.dialogue: have willingly given it to her.

labels.visual_event: Justine on a straw pallet, eyes swollen. Elizabeth and Victor enter. Justine rises and falls into Elizabeth's arms.

next_beat.dialogue: I do not fear to die. That pang is past.

labels.setting: EXT. SHORES OF LAKE GENEVA - DAY
labels.visual_event: Weeks of gloom. Victor wanders alone, unable to rest. The family removes to Belrive. He takes the boat at night and drifts under the stars, sometimes tempted by the water's peace.
He pauses on a winding, rain streaking his face.

prev_beat.dialogue: If our impulses were confined to hunger, thirst, and desire, we might be nearly …

next_beat.dialogue: We rest; a dream has power to poison sleep. We rise; one wandering thought pollu…

labels.visual_event: Victor's rage falters into a bitter necessity of hearing.

prev_beat.dialogue: Oh, praise the eternal justice of man!

next_beat.dialogue: Come to the hut upon the mountain. The sun is yet high in the heavens;

labels.setting: EXT. HOVEL BESIDE THE COTTAGE - NIGHT - FLASHBACK
labels.visual_event: A mean shed built against a cottage of neat plainness. The Creature crawls in, finds it empty of occupant, and lies on straw, grateful for shelter.
Morning. Agatha opens the door-astonished at the great pile of wood. Felix joins her; surprise breaks his habitual sadness. He does not go to the forest that day.

labels.setting: EXT. POOL IN THE WOODS - DAY - FLASHBACK
labels.visual_event: Thaw. The Creature comes upon a transparent pool. He leans to drink-and starts back from his own reflection. Yellow eyes. Scarred yellow skin. Enormous frame. Hideous proportions. He turns his face away, filled with despondence and mortification.

next_beat.dialogue: Alas! I did not yet entirely know the fatal effects of this miserable deformity.

labels.visual_event: Autumn light. DE LACEY sits alone with his guitar. He plays a mournful air, then lays it aside. A knock.

next_beat.dialogue: Who is there? Come in.

labels.setting: INT. HOVEL BESIDE THE COTTAGE - NIGHT - FLASHBACK
labels.visual_event: The Creature howls into the dark.

next_beat.dialogue: Cursed, cursed creator! Why did I live?

labels.setting: EXT. BARN NEAR GENEVA - NIGHT - FLASHBACK
labels.visual_event: Justine asleep in the barn, exhausted from searching. The Creature sees her-sees a way to wound his creator further. He plants the miniature in the folds of her dress and flees.

labels.setting: INT. HUT ON THE MOUNTAIN - DAY
labels.visual_event: The Creature departs over the ice with the speed of lightning. Victor is left alone in the hut as the sun sets behind the snowy precipices.

prev_beat.dialogue: I shall be with you on your wedding-night.

labels.visual_event: Victor pauses over the unfinished female. Imagination shows him a race of devils; the female refusing the compact; two monsters loose upon the world. He looks up. At the window-the Creature's face, a ghastly grin. In a frenzy Victor tears the half-finished creature to pieces. The work of months destroyed in moments. Outside, a howl of rage. The Creature bursts into the hut.

next_beat.dialogue: Shall each man find a wife for his bosom, and each beast have his mate, and I be

labels.setting: EXT. SEA VOYAGE TO GENEVA - DAY
labels.visual_event: Father and son on deck. Victor is a shadow. Alphonse tries to speak of Elizabeth, of hope. Victor hears only the Creature's promise.

next_beat.dialogue: I shall be with you on your wedding-night.

labels.setting: INT. VILLA AT EVIAN - BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT
labels.visual_event: Elizabeth retires to the chamber. Victor paces the outer rooms and passages, pistol in hand, determined to meet his enemy alone. A dreadful scream from the bedroom. Victor rushes in. Elizabeth lies flung across the bed, murdered-the murderous mark of the fiend's grasp on her neck. Her golden hair spilled. Her face half-covered. At the open window, for an instant, the Creature's grinning face in a flash of lightning-then gone into the night. Victor fires into the dark. Emptiness. He falls upon Elizabeth's body in helpless agony.
Victor springs up, but the Creature is already fled.

prev_beat.dialogue: I am satisfied, miserable wretch! You have determined to live, and I am satisfie…

labels.setting: EXT. ARCTIC SEA - PACK ICE - DAY
labels.visual_event: The ice closes harder about the ship. The crew, led by Willoughby's anxious courage and Fletcher's quiet concern, beg Walton to turn south when the floes open.

next_beat.dialogue: Sir-the men will not perish for glory. When the passage clears, we must return.

labels.visual_event: His eyes close. A last breath. Victor Frankenstein dies. Walton weeps over the body of his friend.

prev_beat.dialogue: Yet why do I say this? I have myself been blasted in these hopes, yet another ma…
